facade_description: 52 Oxford St presents a row-house facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a gable, consistent with its house-form, row, ontario cottage typology. The facade reads primarily as mixed in a white-painted rear facade with brown trim and simple porch framing palette, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 1, 3 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 1 principal entrance. Character-defining facade elements include original windows, cornice. HCD note: Each property has value as a nineteenth-century Ontario Cottage House-form building that reflects early workers’ housing within the District.
